channel.title: Semantics on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Semantics on emsenn.net

item.title: Rigid Designation
item.description:
Kripke argues that some terms refer to the same object in every possible world where that object exists. Such terms are rigid designators.

Rigid designation separates a term’s reference from descriptive content that may vary across worlds.

item.title: Semantic and Semiotic Context
item.description:
The interactive semioverse draws on both semantic and semiotic traditions, specifically those that align with a relational ontology — where relations are prior to entities. The mathematical structures correspond to concepts in Peircean semiotics: indexicality, habit, unlimited semiosis, and the pragmatic maxim.

Semantics

The Heyting algebra HH provides the semantic domain — a constructive logic of meanings. The implication a⇒ba \Rightarrow b captures constructive entailment; the modal closure jj formalizes habit-formation; the trace GG formalizes semiotic provenance. These are the semantic structures that the interactive layer operates on.

item.title: Sense and Reference
item.description:
Frege distinguishes between sense (mode of presentation) and reference (the thing referred to). Sense provides the cognitive route by which a term picks out its referent.

This distinction helps explain how two expressions can refer to the same object while conveying different information.

item.title: Semantics, Syntax, and Semiotics
item.description:
Semantics, Syntax, and Semiotics

Three Lenses on Meaningful Structure

This note clarifies how syntax, semantics, and semiotics relate to each other, and how each one shows up in the Semiotic Publisher and the Semiotic Universe.

It is intentionally practical: the goal is to help you read and write vault content in a way that makes the most of the semiotic tooling.

1. Syntax: Shape and Well‑Formedness

Syntax is the study of formal shape.

item.title: Semiotic Markdown
item.description:
Semiotic Markdown Specification

This specification describes how Markdown files, as ingested by the semiotic publisher [@semiotic-publisher], are interpreted as concepts in the Semiotic Universe [@semiotic-universe] and its Stewardable Semiotic Concept Universe extension [@stewardable-semiotic-concept-universe], and how their structure is mapped into atoms, fragments, and publication views.

Normative statements in this document use “MUST”, “SHOULD”, and “MAY” in the conventional requirements-language sense.

1. File Model

  • Throughout this specification, the loader denotes the Markdown‑to‑concept stage of the semiotic publisher [@semiotic-publisher].
